subject: English

lesson.story_mission: Help the Reading Ranger complete the Short Vowel Word Families trail.
lesson.objective: I can practice short vowel word families with careful listening, reading, or writing.
lesson.concept_explanation: This mission teaches short vowel word families using child-friendly models and short practice steps.
lesson.common_misconception: Learners may answer from a first clue instead of checking the whole word, sentence, or story.
lesson.real_world_connection: Readers and writers use this skill in books, signs, notes, and classroom talk.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read or listen carefully, use the model, then choose or write the answer.
lesson.mini_lesson: First study the model. Then say the sounds or words slowly. Last, check that your answer matches the prompt.

audio.text: Listen, then say it with me.
audio.label: Listen

worked_examples.text: Watch: Use the word_family_sort model, name the important part, then answer.
Demo: Try one with help before independent practice.

hint_ladder: Listen for the target sound or word part.
Check your answer with the model.

story.text: Listen to this page. Today you will practice short vowel word families in Skill World. This mission helps you read like a careful first grader. Check the vowel sound and the ending letters before you sort the word. Press Start Mission when you are ready.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This page teaches short vowel word families. Read the vowel and ending chunk together. The word-family card shows words that share the same rime, like a vowel plus ending letters. Check the vowel sound and the ending letters before you sort the word. Press Next to continue.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: This page shows a worked example for Short Vowel Word Families. The word-family card shows words that share the same rime, like a vowel plus ending letters. First, listen carefully to the model. Next, think about what the question is asking. Then compare the choices or words before deciding. Press Next to continue.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This page lets you try short vowel word families with help. Read the vowel and ending chunk together. If you feel unsure, use the model and say the sounds, word, or sentence again. Check the vowel sound and the ending letters before you sort the word. Press Next to continue.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: This practice page helps you use short vowel word families on your own. The question may ask you to read, listen, choose, or write. Check the vowel sound and the ending letters before you sort the word. Press Read Question to hear the question, then choose your answer when you are ready.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: This challenge page gives you a little harder practice with short vowel word families. Read or listen to the whole item before you answer. Use the card, sentence, passage, or picture as evidence. Press Read Question if you want to hear the question, then use the hint if you need help.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: This checkpoint page checks how well you can use short vowel word families. Take your time and listen for the important clue. Check the vowel sound and the ending letters before you sort the word. Press Read Question to hear the item, answer carefully, then continue.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: This badge page celebrates your work on short vowel word families. Think about one sound, word, sentence, or story clue you used today. Good readers listen, look back, and try again when something is tricky. Press Next to see your profile.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This profile page shows your Skill World progress. You practiced short vowel word families and used reading strategies like listening, looking closely, and checking your answer. Keep practicing these skills in books and writing. You can go back to the hub when you are finished.
profile.label: Read This Page
